name: Snov.io
what_it_does: All-in-one sales prospecting platform combining email finding, verification, drip campaign automation, and CRM features to support the full outbound sales workflow from lead discovery to outreach.
use_cases: Finding and verifying email addresses for prospects from domain names or LinkedIn profiles
Running drip email campaigns with automated follow-up sequences to discovered leads
Verifying email lists in bulk before outreach to reduce bounce rates and protect sender reputation
Enriching contact data by finding emails associated with company domains at scale
Building prospecting pipelines that combine email discovery, verification, and outreach in one tool
not_for: Enterprise sales teams needing advanced multi-channel sequences (email + phone + LinkedIn) — Outreach or Reply.io are better suited
Teams with existing verified contact lists who only need outreach tooling
High-volume transactional or marketing email delivery
Organizations requiring HIPAA compliance or sensitive data handling guarantees
best_when: A sales or growth team needs to build prospect lists from scratch, verify email accuracy, and immediately run outreach campaigns — all in a single integrated workflow.
avoid_when: You already have clean verified contact lists and only need a sequence/outreach tool, or you need enterprise-grade multi-channel engagement.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow — client ID and client secret are exchanged for an access token. Tokens have limited lifetime and must be refreshed. No granular scopes — all tokens have full account access. Client credentials obtained from the Snov.io API settings page.

pricing.notes: Credit-based pricing for email finding and verification. Email campaign sending is based on recipient limits. Credits and recipients refresh monthly. Free tier suitable for testing but limited for production use.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses return JSON with status and message fields. Error messages are human-readable but not consistently structured — codes are not standardized across endpoints. Token expiration errors are clear but refresh workflow is not well-documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support. Duplicate email find requests consume credits each time. Agents should cache results or check for existing contacts before making new find requests.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth client credentials flow requires token refresh — agents must implement refresh logic as tokens expire; not documented with clear expiry times
Email finding operations consume credits even for invalid or unverifiable emails — agents with retry logic can exhaust credit budget quickly
Email verification results include confidence scores but no clear threshold is documented for safe-to-send determination
No webhooks available — agents must poll campaign status endpoints to check progress
Prospect list operations (add, remove) are asynchronous — status must be polled rather than read from response
GDPR unsubscribe handling removes contacts from Snov.io lists but agents must propagate this to other systems manually
API rate limits are not clearly documented; hitting limits returns 429 without clear Retry-After headers on all endpoints
Credit balance depletion silently fails email find operations rather than returning a billing error on some endpoints

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth 2.0 client credentials is marginally stronger than plain API key but provides no scope granularity — tokens have full account access. Client secret must be stored securely by the integrating application. No audit logs or token management controls via API. GDPR data processing agreement available. Email data for found contacts raises additional GDPR considerations for EU-based targets.
